commands.repair_reason: Protected maintainer handling and missing contributor real behavior proof make this a human-review item, not an automated repair or cleanup candidate.; Cleared: The diff only changes local CLI option decoding and a focused unit test; it does not add dependencies, workflow execution, secret handling, or package-resolution surface. (sha=978ce2859e5b6df3bfdd4eebfe772f2ddedd29ec)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes export-trajectory request decoding so absent or blank encoded fields do not overwrite directly supplied options, and adds focused regression coverage.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection rather than an executed failing command: current main decodes an omitted encoded `sessionKey` to an empty string and spreads it over direct CLI options. The CLI can pass direct options and `requestJsonBase64` together.
commands.review_followup: Protected maintainer handling and missing contributor real behavior proof make this a human-review item, not an automated repair or cleanup candidate.

target.title: fix(export): preserve explicit trajectory session keys
target.body:
## Summary
- omit absent/blank decoded trajectory export request fields instead of merging empty placeholders
- preserve direct CLI options such as `sessionKey` and `store` when the encoded request only supplies another option
- add coverage for encoded request merging with explicit direct options

Fixes #83282.

## Real behavior proof
- Behavior or issue addressed: encoded trajectory export requests that omit `sessionKey` should not erase an explicit direct `sessionKey`/`store` option.
- Real environment tested: WSL Ubuntu OpenClaw worktree at `/root/src/openclaw-branches/fix-export-trajectory-request-83282`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/export-trajectory.test.ts` and `pnpm check:changed`.
- Evidence after fix (terminal capture):

```text
RUN  v4.1.6 /root/src/openclaw-branches/fix-export-trajectory-request-83282

✓ commands  ../../src/commands/export-trajectory.test.ts (4 tests) 125ms

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  4 passed (4)

[check:changed] src/commands/export-trajectory.test.ts: core test
[check:changed] src/commands/export-trajectory.ts: core production
Import cycle check: 0 runtime value cycle(s).
```

- Observed result after fix: the regression test keeps `sessionKey: "agent:main:telegram:direct:123"` and `store: "/tmp/direct-store.json"` when the encoded request only supplies `output`; the command reaches the expected “Session not found” path instead of the `--session-key is required` path.
- What was not tested: a live trajectory export against a real session file.
- Before evidence (terminal/code capture from `origin/main`):

```text
src/commands/export-trajectory.ts before this patch decoded every request with:
  sessionKey: readOptionalString(request.sessionKey) ?? "",
...
and merged decoded values after direct opts:
  ...opts,
  ...decodeExportTrajectoryRequest(encoded),

An encoded request without sessionKey therefore overwrote a direct sessionKey with "".
```

## Root Cause
- Root cause: decoded request options included empty placeholders for absent optional fields, then spread them over already-defined CLI options.
- Missing detection / guardrail: no regression coverage for mixing direct options with an encoded request that only supplies a different field.
- Contributing context: the decoder returned a partial options object but populated `sessionKey` even when the encoded JSON omitted it.

## Regression Test Plan
- Coverage level that should have caught this: unit test.
- Target test or file: `src/commands/export-trajectory.test.ts`.
- Scenario the test should lock in: encoded request fields that are absent or blank do not replace direct CLI options.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the bug is in option decoding/merge behavior before filesystem export work begins.

## Validation
- `CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/export-trajectory.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
